column1: Copied from the latest tailored AI Readiness content pack.

column3: Screens inbound leads against ICP rules, source quality, budget/timeline clues, and response urgency so sales only chases leads worth fast follow-up.
column6: New form fill, website enquiry, WhatsApp lead, or SDR handoff
column7: Sales teams spend too much time reading weak leads manually and good leads wait too long for first response.
column10: Lead form fields, source campaign, website notes, prior contact history
column11: Priority score, qualification summary, next-step recommendation, and owner routing
column14: CRM lead record and assignment workflow
column15: Higher lead-to-opportunity conversion and faster speed-to-lead
column16: Qualified lead rate and median first-response time
column19: Lead source fields, ICP rules, and routing owners must already be defined

column3: Creates first-draft outreach emails or LinkedIn messages using account context, industry pain points, and recent buyer signals.
column7: Reps lose time writing repetitive first-touch messages and quality varies heavily by seller.
column10: CRM account data, website summary, campaign angle, rep notes
column11: Personalized outreach draft with subject line, opening hook, and CTA
column14: CRM, sales engagement platform, or email draft workflow
column15: More meetings booked per rep hour and stronger outbound consistency
column16: Meeting-booked rate from outbound and draft acceptance/edit ratio
column19: Core account/contact data and approved messaging guardrails must exist

column3: Builds first-draft proposals, statements of work, or scope summaries from discovery notes, pricing inputs, and standard service components.
column6: Opportunity enters proposal stage or scope revision requested
column7: Proposal turnaround is slow, senior staff rewrite similar scopes repeatedly, and pricing context gets lost between teams.
column10: Discovery notes, approved service catalogue, pricing assumptions, case studies, templates
column11: Proposal draft, scope summary, assumptions list, and missing-input prompts
column14: Proposal template, document workspace, and CRM opportunity record
column15: Faster proposal turnaround with less senior delivery time consumed
column16: Proposal cycle time and win-rate on proposals delivered within SLA
column19: Approved proposal templates, service catalogue, and pricing logic must be current

column2: Meeting Summary and Action Capture Assistant
column3: Summarizes internal or client meetings into decisions, owners, risks, and next actions that can be pushed into task workflows.
column6: Meeting transcript or notes become available
column7: Teams lose actions after meetings, notes are inconsistent, and follow-up depends on whoever took the best notes.
column10: Meeting transcript, calendar metadata, agenda, manual notes
column11: Decision summary, action list, owner assignments, and unresolved-question log
column14: Meeting platform, project tool, or task tracker
column15: Less follow-up leakage and faster execution after meetings
column16: Action completion rate and time from meeting end to action circulation
column19: Meeting recording/transcript access and action-owner conventions must exist

column2: Support Ticket Triage and Routing Assistant
column3: Reads new support requests, classifies issue type and urgency, and routes the case to the correct queue with a recommended first response.
column6: New email, portal ticket, or chat escalation is created
column7: Support teams waste time manually sorting tickets, urgent issues are missed, and queue quality depends on agent experience.
column10: Ticket text, customer tier, product/service context, prior case history
column11: Issue category, urgency flag, queue routing, and suggested acknowledgement draft
column14: Helpdesk queue, inbox routing rules, or CRM case module
column15: Lower first-response delays and better queue quality
column16: First-response SLA attainment and misroute rate
column19: Ticket categories, escalation rules, and queue ownership must already be defined

column3: Answers internal staff questions using approved SOPs, policy documents, service playbooks, and common issue resolutions.
column6: User asks a policy, SOP, or process question
column7: Staff rely on tribal knowledge, search across scattered files, or interrupt senior team members for routine answers.
column10: Approved SOPs, FAQs, policy docs, service manuals, templates
column11: Contextual answer with cited source document snippets and escalation cue if confidence is low
column14: Chat interface, intranet, or internal help workflow
column15: Less interruption of senior staff and faster task execution
column16: Question resolution time and reduction in repeat internal queries
column19: Source documents must be current, approved, and access-controlled

column3: Checks onboarding packs for missing documents, inconsistent fields, or obvious handoff risks before the team moves the client live.
column6: Customer submits onboarding forms or compliance documents
column7: Onboarding teams manually check packs line by line, miss missing items, and discover problems late in the process.
column10: Application forms, contracts, ID/compliance docs, implementation checklist
column11: Missing-item checklist, inconsistency flags, and readiness status suggestion
column14: Client onboarding checklist and case workflow
column15: Shorter onboarding cycle time and fewer late-stage rework loops
column16: First-pass completeness rate and onboarding cycle time

column2: Invoice and Remittance Query Classification Assistant
column3: Classifies inbound accounts-receivable emails into dispute, remittance, proof-of-delivery, copy-invoice, or payment-delay types and suggests the right next queue.
column6: Customer finance email arrives in shared AR inbox
column7: Finance staff spend time manually reading repetitive emails and urgent cash-impacting issues are not surfaced early enough.
column10: Inbound email, invoice reference, account status, prior dispute history
column11: Query type, priority flag, next-owner queue, and response guidance
column14: Shared finance inbox, AR case board, or ERP notes
column15: Faster cash-resolution workflow and reduced inbox backlog
column16: Inbox aging and average resolution time for payment-related queries
column19: Common query categories and escalation rules must already be agreed

column3: Ranks overdue accounts by likelihood of recovery, customer risk, and urgency so teams focus attention where cash impact is highest.
column6: Daily or weekly overdue debtor review
column7: Collectors often work the debtor list oldest-first instead of highest-impact-first, leaving easy cash and high-risk debt unattended.
column10: Aging data, payment history, dispute status, customer tier, promise-to-pay notes
column11: Priority queue, recovery rationale, and recommended follow-up style
column14: AR aging report, collector worklist, or CRM notes
column15: Better cash collection focus and lower aged debt buildup
column16: Cash collected from prioritized accounts and reduction in >90-day debt
column19: Clean aging data and collector action history must exist

column3: Turns pipeline, bookings, revenue, margin, or operational KPI movements into leadership-ready commentary that explains what changed and why it matters.
column6: Weekly or monthly reporting cycle begins
column7: Analysts spend hours rewriting commentary that repeats similar themes, while leadership still wants a clear narrative not just numbers.
column10: KPI tables, pipeline data, budget vs actuals, prior period comparisons
column11: Narrative summary, variance commentary, and follow-up questions for review
column14: Reporting pack, BI output, or board update draft
column15: Faster management reporting with more consistent interpretation of variances
column16: Reporting cycle time and percentage of commentary accepted with minor edits
column19: Baseline KPI definitions and clean source reports must already exist

column2: Sales Call Review and Deal Risk Assistant

column11: Coaching summary, deal-risk note, objection themes, and next-step gap flag
column14: Conversation-intelligence tool or CRM opportunity notes
column15: Improved deal inspection quality and better manager coaching leverage
column16: Manager review coverage and late-stage deal slippage rate

column2: Renewal and Churn Risk Review Assistant

column7: Success teams often find risk too late and account reviews depend on whoever knows the customer best.

column11: Risk summary, likely churn drivers, and recommended intervention type
column14: CSM account review, CRM renewal board, or customer health dashboard

column3: Guides staff through approved internal processes such as leave, procurement, incident logging, or service handoffs using current policy language.

column14: Internal portal, HR helpdesk, or workflow launcher

column3: Flags orders, jobs, or service cases that break expected patterns such as missing fields, unusual delays, repeated rework, or breached handoffs.
